TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Goatmen Raiders Faction.

Translations.Comment: Ziegenmenschen = goatmen (beast-men with goat features). Plünderer = raiders. Asaltantes conveys the raiding/pillaging nature.
Steinbock = ibex (mountain goat). Fierce warriors. Íbice is the Spanish term for ibex, emphasizing their mountain-dwelling nature.
Standard goat-men warriors. Savage and brutal. Direct translation maintaining consistency with faction name.
Mischling = mongrel/mixed breed. Jauría emphasizes the pack/horde nature, used for both beasts and rabid armies.
Mongrel troops armed with spears. Lanceros is the standard term for spear-armed troops in Spanish fantasy contexts.
Plünder- prefix = raiding. Fast attack chariot. Carro de Asalto conveys rapid assault tactics.
Wilde = feral/wild. Jagdhunde = hunting hounds. Sabuesos Salvajes emphasizes both the hunting nature and feral aspect.
Plänkler = skirmishers. Light troops for harassment. Caballeros Ligeros is the classical term for light skirmishing cavalry.
Standard German for centaurs. Half-man, half-horse. Direct translation is identical to German.
Standard German for minotaurs. Bull-headed warriors. Direct translation matching German cognate.
Flying monsters allied with the beast-men. Standard Spanish term for mythological harpies.
Wilder = feral/wild. Untamed giant serving the beast-men. Simple, direct translation fitting wargame terminology.
Tribal magic user. Dark rituals and curses. Chamán is standard Spanish for shaman; maintains consistency with faction naming.
Langhorn = longhorn. Elite warrior with impressive horns. Cuernilargo captures the impressive horn aesthetic while maintaining fantasy wargame tone.
Mighty minotaur leader. Powerful hero. Señor conveys nobility and command authority befitting a faction hero unit.
